question: Recall that event $A$ is cat ownership and event $B$ is dog ownership. We have established $\operatorname{Pr}(B \mid A)= 0.4286, \operatorname{Pr}( ar{A})=1-\operatorname{Pr}(A)=1-0.35=0.65$, and $\operatorname{Pr}(B$ and $ ar{A})=0.15$. By conditional probability, $\operatorname{Pr}(B \mid  ar{A})= rac{\operatorname{Pr}(B 	ext { and }  ar{A})}{\operatorname{Pr}( ar{A})}= rac{.15}{.65}=0.2308$.
answer: By the total probability rule, $\operatorname{Pr}(B)=[\operatorname{Pr}(B \mid A) 	imes \operatorname{Pr}(A)]+[\operatorname{Pr}(B \mid  ar{A}) 	imes \operatorname{Pr}( ar{A})]= [0.4286 	imes 0.35]+[0.2308 	imes 0.65]=0.15+0.15=0.30$

meta info.caption: Total probability rule (Pet ownership).
meta info.section: 5.5  MORE RULES AND PROPERTIES OF PROBABILITY
